description: Record a Stellar transaction (by hash or simulated envelope) and return the deterministic `Recording` IR. Pass exactly one of `hash` or `envelope_xdr_base64`.
name: record_transaction

NetworkKind.description: Stellar network discriminant. Selects passphrase + default RPC URL.

parameters.description:
`record_transaction` input. Exactly one of `hash` / `envelope_xdr_base64`
must be present; the handler validates this at runtime (the JSON Schema
emits both as optional with a `oneOf`-style description). `rpc_url`
defaults per `network`.

envelope_xdr_base64.description:
Base64-encoded `TransactionEnvelope` XDR to simulate. Mutually
exclusive with [`Self::hash`].

hash.description:
64-char hex transaction hash. Mutually exclusive with
[`Self::envelope_xdr_base64`].

instruction_leeway.description:
Optional `simulateTransaction.resourceConfig.instructionLeeway`
override (in instructions). Honoured only by the simulation path;
stable `stellar-rpc-client 25.1.0` does not expose this on the
stable API surface, so the recorder logs a `tracing::warn!` and
continues with the default budget — see
`oz-policy-recorder/src/recorder.rs` for the no-op contract.

network.description:
Stellar network the transaction belongs to. Selects the default
network passphrase + RPC endpoint when `rpc_url` is omitted.

rpc_url.description:
Optional RPC URL override. Defaults to [`DEFAULT_TESTNET_RPC`] /
[`DEFAULT_MAINNET_RPC`] per `network`.

parameters.title: RecordTransactionInput

description: Synthesize the minimum-rights `PolicySpec` (oz-policy-builder/v1) that would permit the given Recording under the chosen `tightness` and `mode`.
name: synthesize_policy

SynthesisMode.description:
Three-way switch controlling which synthesizer track may run.

* `Auto` — synthesizer is free to compose existing primitives (Track A)
  and / or emit generated policy slots (Track B).
* `ComposeOnly` — synthesizer must succeed using only existing OZ
  primitives (Track A). If the constraints cannot be expressed,
  `E_SYNTH_NOT_EXPRESSIBLE`.
* `CodegenOnly` — synthesizer must emit a `Generated` policy slot for
  every constraint (Track B). Useful for testing the codegen path
  end-to-end against constraints that *could* compose to
  `simple_threshold` etc.

Tightness.description:
Numeric tightness used when scaling observed `i128` constraints.

Per `plan.md` Phase 2 *Implementation → decision tree* §:
* `Exact` — emit the observed value verbatim (×1.0).
* `SmallMargin` — emit observed × 1.1 (10% headroom).
* `Loose` — emit observed × 2.0 (2× headroom).

The scale factors are applied with **decimal-string** `i128` arithmetic
via [`i128::checked_mul`] — never floats — so amounts like
`170141183460469231731687303715884105727` (i128::MAX) round-trip exactly
at `Exact` and surface as [`Error::SynthNotExpressible`] (not as a panic
or silent wrap) on overflow under `SmallMargin` / `Loose`.

parameters.description:
`synthesize_policy` input. Looks up `recording_id` in the store and
drives `oz_policy_core::decision_tree::synthesize` with the typed
options.

delegated_signer.description:
Optional delegated-signer contract address. When set, the
synthesizer emits a single `SignerSpec::Delegated` regardless of
observed signers (see `decision_tree::SynthesisOptions::delegated_signer`).

mode.description:
Which synthesis path is permitted: compose existing primitives,
emit a generated slot, or let the synthesizer choose.

recording_id.description: Recording ID returned by an earlier [`record_transaction`] call.

rule_name.description:
Optional context rule name. Defaults to `"rule-<first-8-chars-of-id>"`
when omitted; the handler clamps the result to `MAX_NAME_SIZE`
(20 bytes) per `docs/oz-internal-shapes.md` §7.

tightness.description: Numeric scaling factor for observed `i128` constraints.

parameters.title: SynthesizePolicyInput

description: Simulate the given spec against its source recording (permit) and the synthesized + caller-supplied deny vectors. Returns a `SimReport`. Always run this before exporting.
name: simulate_policy

ArgValue.description:
Fully decoded `ScVal` mirror. Every Soroban `ScVal` variant maps to one of
these. Large integers serialise as JSON strings to preserve precision;
bytes as hex; addresses as StrKey.

If the Soroban host ever introduces an `ScVal` variant we cannot decode
(none exist today for `stellar-xdr 25.0.0`), the recorder must surface
`Error::RecorderXdrDecodeFailed` rather than emit `Unsupported`.

`Eq` is derived because every contained field type is `Eq`: floats are
not part of the `ScVal` shape, all `String`/`Option<String>`/`Vec<T>` /
integer payloads are `Eq`. This lets downstream consumers (Phase 4
`DenyVector`, set / map collections of recordings) compare values
without falling back to manual comparators.

oneOf.description:
64-bit unsigned. JSON-encoded as a string to avoid loss in
53-bit-mantissa consumers.
64-bit signed. JSON-encoded as a string for the same reason.
Timepoint (seconds since epoch, u64). JSON string.
Duration (seconds, u64). JSON string.
128-bit unsigned. JSON string.
128-bit signed. JSON string.
256-bit unsigned. JSON string.
256-bit signed. JSON string.
Raw bytes, hex-encoded.

UTF-8 string (`ScString`). Note: Soroban allows non-UTF-8 byte payloads
in `ScString`; in that case we fall back to a hex representation here.
Symbol — restricted-alphabet UTF-8.

`ScVec` — `None` distinguishes the empty-marker from `Some(vec![])`
matches the on-chain encoding (`Vec(Option<ScVec>)`).

StrKey-encoded address (`C…` for contracts, `G…` for accounts,
`M…` muxed, `B…` claimable balance, `L…` liquidity pool).

`ScContractInstance` — emitted only when the host stores a contract
instance value (rare in user-facing args).

System-reserved sentinel used in contract-data keys for the instance
pseudo-entry. No payload.
System-reserved nonce key (auth replay protection).

AuthPayload.description:
Auth payload handed to `__check_auth`. Mirrors the on-chain
`AuthPayload` (see `docs/oz-internal-shapes.md` §10) but uses
StrKey-encoded signer addresses + a typed `context_rule_ids` slice so
the type is wire-portable above the `soroban-env-host` boundary.

`JsonSchema` is derived (Phase 5 Stream A) so the MCP `simulate_policy`
tool can publish a structured input schema for `extra_deny_vectors`.

context_rule_ids.description:
Per-context-rule IDs, aligned by index with the `contexts` vector
passed alongside this payload into `invoke_check_auth`.

signer_addresses.description:
StrKey `G…` / `C…` addresses for each signer that participates in
this authorization. Order is preserved to mirror the recording's
signer composition.

DenyVector.description:
One deny vector — a `(payload, contexts)` pair that the harness replays
against `__check_auth` and expects to panic with `expected_error_code`.

Implements `Serialize` + `Deserialize` so the Phase 4 determinism gate
(`generate_deny_vectors_is_byte_equal_for_same_seed`) can JSON-encode two
vectors and compare them as strings, and so the CLI's
`simulate --extra-deny <json>` flag (Stream A's `run.rs`) can round-trip
extra deny vectors through disk. `Eq` is derivable now that
`oz_policy_core::ArgValue` is `Eq` (Phase 4 Stream-A extension; no float
variants exist in the `ScVal` shape — see `host.rs:134-136`).

`JsonSchema` is derived (Phase 5 Stream A) so the MCP `simulate_policy`
tool can publish a structured input schema for `extra_deny_vectors`.

expected_error_code.description:
Discriminant of the `PolicyError` / `*Error` enum variant the harness
expects the policy WASM to panic with.

name.description:
Stable identifier for this vector (`"<primitive>_<mutation>"`).
Required to be byte-equal across two invocations with the same
`(spec, recording, seed)`.

MapEntry.description:
Map entry pair — explicit struct so JSON serialisation is unambiguous and
`schemars` produces a clean schema (tuples land as 2-element arrays which
hide field intent).

TestContext.description:
One decoded `Context::Contract { contract, fn_name, args }` invocation
presented to `__check_auth`. The wrapper translates each `TestContext`
into a host-side `ScVal` shape during [`TestHost::invoke_check_auth`].

`Eq` is derivable because [`ArgValue`] is now `Eq` (Phase 4 Stream-A
extension; safe — no float variants exist in the `ScVal` shape).

`JsonSchema` is derived (Phase 5 Stream A) so the MCP `simulate_policy`
tool can publish a structured input schema for `extra_deny_vectors`.

extra_deny_vectors.description:
Optional caller-supplied deny vectors appended to the generated
boundary-mutation set. Default empty.

recording_id.description:
Recording ID — the simhost replays this against the compiled
policy WASMs as the permit branch.

spec_id.description: Spec ID returned by an earlier [`synthesize_policy`] call.

description: Materialize the spec's compiled Track-B WASM, generated Rust source, and/or signed install envelope (per `format`). Returns inline payloads plus `resource://` URIs.
name: export_policy

AccountRevision.description:
Caller-asserted statement about the target smart-account contract's
release vintage. See module doc-comment for why a user-asserted flag is
the only feasible v1 strategy.

`Serialize` + `Deserialize` + `JsonSchema` are derived (Phase 5 Stream A)
so the MCP `export_policy` tool can accept this discriminator on its
JSON input wire and publish a structured schema for it. The wire form
is the snake_case variant name (`"post_pr_655"`, `"pre_pr_655"`,
`"unknown"`), matching the rest of the policy IR convention.

oneOf.description:
Operator asserts the deployed smart-account WASM came from
`stellar-contracts >= v0.7.0-rc.2` (the first tag containing the
PR-#655 merge commit, per `docs/oz-internal-shapes.md` §8).

Operator asserts the deployed smart-account WASM predates PR-#655.
Always a hard refusal — installing onto a vulnerable account would
expose the user to the sponsor-substitution attack PR-#655 fixed.

Operator does not know. Also a hard refusal in v1, since the v1.1
WASM-hash whitelist (option 1 in `docs/oz-internal-shapes.md` §8) is
not yet wired up.

Render Track-B Rust source for every `Generated` slot. No
sandboxed build, no install envelope.

Render Track-B Rust source AND drive it through the codegen
sandbox so the output carries compiled WASM bytes + SHA-256.
Implies [`ExportFormat::RustSource`].

Build the install envelope only (Track-A path) — no Track-B
codegen, no WASM.
Everything: source + WASM + install envelope.

account_revision.description:
Caller-asserted smart-account release vintage. The installer
refuses anything other than [`AccountRevision::PostPr655`] in v1
(see `docs/oz-internal-shapes.md` §8).

network_passphrase.description: Network passphrase the RPC endpoint is asserted to serve.

rpc_url.description:
Soroban RPC URL — the installer uses it for the
`simulateTransaction` round-trip that fills in
`transactionData` + auth.

smart_account.description:
Target smart-account StrKey `C…` address (where the policy will
install).

source_account.description: Source account StrKey `G…` paying fees + signing the envelope.

spec_id.description: Spec ID returned by an earlier [`synthesize_policy`] call.

description: Verify the on-chain context rule at `(smart_account, context_rule_id)` matches the expected `PolicySpec`. Returns a drift report (empty when matches=true).
name: verify_install

NetworkKind.description: Stellar network discriminant. Selects passphrase + default RPC URL.

context_rule_id.description: Context rule ID assigned by `add_context_rule` at install time.

expected_spec_id.description:
Optional spec_id to compare against. When `None`, the handler
reports the on-chain rule shape but cannot decide drift (the
`matches` field is then `false` with a synthetic drift item
describing the missing-spec condition).

smart_account.description:
Smart-account StrKey `C…` whose on-chain context rule we will
inspect.
